name: Mondariz - Ponte Caldeas Return
region_intro: Touring cycling around Mondariz-Balneario offers routes through a landscape characterized by river valleys, historic bridges, and undulating hills. The region features a mix of forested areas and open terrain, providing varied scenery for cyclists. Many routes follow the course of rivers, offering relatively flat sections interspersed with gradual climbs. This area is suitable for exploring the natural and cultural heritage of Galicia by bike. Best touring cycling routes around Mondariz-Balneario The most popular touring cycling route is Mondariz - Ponte Caldeas Return, a 37.5 miles (60.4 km) trail that takes 4 hours 34 minutes to complete. This difficult route features significant elevation gain through varied terrain. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is Ponte de Fillaboa – Bridge of the Partitions loop from O Condado, a difficult 33.3 miles (53.5 km) path. This route traverses riverine landscapes and includes notable bridges. Local touring cyclists also love the Remedios Bridge loop from Meirol, a 17.2 miles (27.6 km) trail leading through river valleys and past historic bridges, often completed in about 1 hour 49 minutes. Touring cycling around Mondariz-Balneario is defined by river valleys, historic bridges, and undulating forested hills. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult routes, catering to various ability levels. The routes in Mondariz-Balneario are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.9 stars from more than 20 reviews. More than 300 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Mondariz-Balneario's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available in Mondariz-Balneario?
A: There are over 40 touring cycling routes around Mondariz-Balneario, offering a diverse range of experiences for cyclists of all levels.

Q: Are there any easy touring cycling routes suitable for beginners or families?
A: Yes, while many routes are moderate to difficult, there is at least one easy touring cycling route available. The region's landscape features river valleys with relatively flat sections, which can be suitable for less experienced riders or families. For a moderate option that's not too long, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/40366588" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;BTT Picaraña loop from Lamazáns&lt;/a&gt;, which is 13.6 km.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Mondariz-Balneario?
A: The region of Galicia, where Mondariz-Balneario is located, generally experiences mild winters and warm summers. Spring and autumn often provide the most comfortable temperatures for cycling, with pleasant weather and lush landscapes. Summer can also be enjoyable, especially along routes that offer shade from forested areas.

Q: Are there any circular touring cycling routes in the area?
A: Yes, many routes in Mondariz-Balneario are designed as loops, allowing you to start and finish in the same location. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/33760550" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ponte de Fillaboa – Bridge of the Partitions loop from O Condado&lt;/a&gt;, which is a challenging 53.5 km route traversing riverine landscapes.

Q: What kind of scenery can I expect on the touring cycling routes?
A: The routes around Mondariz-Balneario are characterized by river valleys, historic bridges, and undulating hills. You'll cycle through a mix of forested areas and open terrain, offering varied scenery that highlights the natural and cultural heritage of Galicia.

Q: What are some interesting landmarks or natural attractions to see along the routes?
A: The area boasts several points of interest. You might encounter historic bridges that span the rivers, or natural features like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5518672" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Barragán River&lt;/a&gt;. For those interested in history, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2886707" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;A Coutada Petroglyphs&lt;/a&gt; offer a glimpse into ancient times. Mountain peaks such as &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/2532948" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Monte de A Picaraña&lt;/a&gt; also provide scenic backdrops.

Q: How difficult are the touring cycling routes in Mondariz-Balneario?
A: The routes vary significantly in difficulty. There is 1 easy route, 12 moderate routes, and 30 difficult routes. This means you can find options ranging from relatively flat sections along rivers to challenging paths with significant elevation gain through undulating hills. For a demanding ride, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e950359747" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mondariz - Ponte Caldeas Return&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 60.4 km with considerable climbing.

Q: Are there any long-distance touring cycling options?
A: Yes, for those seeking longer rides, there are several routes exceeding 50 km. The longest route listed is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38163672" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Remedios Bridge – Cabalón Bridge loop from Ponteareas&lt;/a&gt;, which spans 77.1 km and typically takes over 5 hours to complete, featuring significant elevation changes.

Q: What do other touring cyclists say about the routes in Mondariz-Balneario?
A: The touring cycling routes in Mondariz-Balneario are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.9 stars from over 20 reviews. Cyclists often praise the varied terrain, the beauty of the river valleys, and the presence of historic bridges that add character to the rides.

Q: Are there any moderate touring cycling routes available?
A: Yes, there are 12 moderate touring cycling routes in the area. These routes offer a good balance of challenge and accessibility, often featuring undulating terrain without the extreme climbs of the more difficult paths. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/6951161" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Remedios Bridge loop from Meirol&lt;/a&gt;, a 27.6 km trail through river valleys.
tips: Mondariz is the town above the hot springs, thermal resort, Mondariz – Balneario. The town has a few good coffee shops and eateries, a small square and a good traditional market.
